sections.conclusion: Mrs. Bridges would not be disqualified from decisions in which Madrone provided services to the City because: (1) a Planning Commission decision would not have a direct effect on Mrs. Bridges' income; (2) there is no nexus between the decision and the purpose for which she receives income since she did not perform consulting services on the project; and (3) Madrone was selected based on objective criteria (experience and expertise) rather than its success before the Planning Commission, so the decision would not materially affect the consultant's business.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Planning Commissioner with financial ties to a consulting firm (earning >$250/year) must disqualify herself from matters involving that firm when she personally did not work on the matter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded Mrs. Bridges is not disqualified because the Planning Commission decision would not have a material financial effect on either her income or Madrone Associates' business, as Madrone was selected on objective qualifications rather than its ability to influence outcomes.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with moderate quality; some text degradation and artifacts present. Response letter dated December 27, 1979 from FPPC to requesting attorney. Includes incoming request letter (December 7, 1979) at bottom providing case facts.

CONCLUSION: Mrs. Bridges would not be disqualified from decisions in which Madrone provided services to the City because: (1) a Planning Commission decision would not have a direct effect on Mrs. Bridges' income; (2) there is no nexus between the decision and the purpose for which she receives income since she did not perform consulting services on the project; and (3) Madrone was selected based on objective criteria (experience and expertise) rather than its success before the Planning Commission, so the decision would not materially affect the consultant's business.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter determining whether Planning Commissioner Cecilia Bridges must disqualify herself from matters involving consulting firm Madrone Associates, from which she receives income, when she personally did not work on the matter.
